update.headline: Orion speeds toward the Moon on Flight Day 3 following successful Translunar Injection.
details: The crew of Orion Integrity completed a 5-minute 55-second TLI burn to reach escape velocity.
Mission activities include human health research (AVATAR study) and space adaptation exercises.
Engineers resolved minor issues with a toilet system controller and a water tank valve position.
Orion is on a free-return trajectory with a lunar flyby scheduled for April 6.
sources: Artemis II Flight Journal - Flight Day 3
update.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits.
update.lastEvent: Successful completion of the Translunar Injection (TLI) burn at 23:49 UTC on April 2.
update.nextEvent: First Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1) burn to refine Orion's path.

milestones.label: Launch
milestones.detail: SLS liftoff from Pad 39B

milestones.label: Core Stage Separation
milestones.detail: MECO & core stage jettison

milestones.label: Solar Array Deploy
milestones.detail: Four Orion solar wings extended

milestones.label: Perigee Raise
milestones.detail: Orion performed perigee raise and apogee raise burns.

milestones.label: Proximity Ops
milestones.detail: Crew successfully demonstrated manual piloting of Orion near the ICPS upper stage.

milestones.label: Translunar Injection
milestones.detail: The 5-minute 55-second burn successfully accelerated the spacecraft to escape velocity of 24,500 mph.

milestones.label: ICPS Separation
milestones.detail: Upper stage jettisoned

milestones.label: AVATAR Scientific Payload Checkout
milestones.detail: Testing 'organ-on-a-chip' technology to study deep space radiation effects on bone marrow cells.

milestones.label: Earth Photography by Crew
milestones.detail: Crew captures high-resolution imagery of Earth from deep space.

milestones.label: Communications Blackout Behind Moon
milestones.detail: Temporary loss of communications with Earth as Orion passes behind the Moon.

milestones.label: Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1)
milestones.detail: An 8-second burn to refine path for lunar flyby.

milestones.label: Lunar Sphere Of Influence
milestones.detail: Entry into the Moon's gravitational sphere of influence.

milestones.label: Human Distance Record
milestones.detail: Crew expected to surpass the Apollo 13 record distance from Earth.

milestones.label: Farthest Distance from Earth Record
milestones.detail: Orion surpasses the maximum distance from Earth of previous Apollo missions.

milestones.label: Lunar Surface Observation
milestones.detail: Crew performs visual observations and photography of the lunar surface.

milestones.label: Lunar Flyby
milestones.detail: Closest lunar approach and flyby maneuvers.

milestones.label: Human Distance Record
milestones.detail: Expected to reach 252,799 miles from Earth, breaking the Apollo 13 record.

milestones.label: Earthrise Photo Ops
milestones.detail: Crew photographs Earthrise

milestones.label: ISS Crew Conversation
milestones.detail: Scheduled outreach event with crew members currently aboard the International Space Station.

milestones.label: Return Coast
milestones.detail: Free-return trajectory to Earth

milestones.label: Splashdown
milestones.detail: Final splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego.

milestones.label: Re-entry
milestones.detail: Skip re-entry at ~5,000°F

history.headline: Orion speeds toward the Moon on Flight Day 3 following successful Translunar Injection.
details: The crew of Orion Integrity completed a 5-minute 55-second TLI burn to reach escape velocity.
Mission activities include human health research (AVATAR study) and space adaptation exercises.
Engineers resolved minor issues with a toilet system controller and a water tank valve position.
Orion is on a free-return trajectory with a lunar flyby scheduled for April 6.
sources: Artemis II Flight Journal - Flight Day 3
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Successful completion of the Translunar Injection (TLI) burn at 23:49 UTC on April 2.
history.nextEvent: First Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1) burn to refine Orion's path.

history.headline: Artemis II crew enters deep space for the first time in over 50 years after successful Translunar Injection.
details: Orion spacecraft 'Integrity' is transiting toward the Moon following a 5-minute 50-second TLI burn on April 2.
Crew is testing critical hardware including the flywheel exercise device and Orion Optical Communications System.
Minor issues with cabin pressure indications and waste management have been successfully resolved by the crew and ground teams.
The crew is currently preparing for the Outbound Trajectory Correction-1 (OTC-1) burn to refine their lunar approach.
sources: NASA Artemis II Mission Status Report
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and spirits.
history.lastEvent: Completion of the Translunar Injection (TLI) burn on April 2 at 23:49Z.
history.nextEvent: Outbound Trajectory Correction-1 (OTC-1) burn scheduled for 22:49Z.

history.headline: Artemis II crew officially moonbound after successful Translunar Injection burn.
details: Orion executed a critical 5 minute and 55 second TLI burn to break free from Earth's gravitational pull.
Crew is currently performing medical demonstrations, including CPR simulations and space adaptation assessments.
The Waste Management System (toilet) was successfully troubleshot on Day 1 and is fully operational.
Orion is following a free-return trajectory toward the Moon with a lunar flyby expected on April 6.
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in good health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Translunar Injection (TLI) burn completed at 23:49Z on April 2, 2026.
history.nextEvent: Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1) scheduled for April 3 at 22:49Z.

history.currentPhase: Outbound coasting trajectory towards the Moon
history.headline: The Artemis II mission is performing well following the successful completion of the translunar injection burn.
details: Crew is in good health and has successfully acclimated to the space environment.
The Integrity spacecraft is on a precise trajectory toward the Moon.
Operations currently focus on cabin configuration and spacecraft system checkouts.
A minor operational issue with the spacecraft's waste management system was successfully resolved.
history.crewStatus: All four crew members are in good health and have completed their first deep space rest period.
history.lastEvent: Translunar Injection (TLI) burn completed on April 2, 2026.
history.nextEvent: Outbound trajectory correction (OTC) burn on April 3, 2026.

history.headline: Artemis II crew is healthy and successfully en route to the Moon on Flight Day 3.
details: The Orion spacecraft is coasting toward the Moon at a velocity of 19,512 km/h.
Crew members conducted successful medical demonstrations today, including CPR and choking-response procedures.
All systems are reported operational, including the Orion flywheel exercise device and the recently repaired toilet system.
The crew is approximately 214,626 km from Earth and 176,145 km from the Moon.
history.crewStatus: All four crew members are in excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: First trajectory correction burn (OTC-1) to fine-tune the lunar path.
history.nextEvent: Lunar sphere of influence entry on Flight Day 5.

history.currentPhase: Orion is currently on a translunar trajectory toward the Moon following a successful TLI burn.
history.headline: Artemis II crew cruises toward the Moon after completing the critical Translunar Injection maneuver.
details: The 5-minute and 55-second TLI burn was successfully executed at 7:49 p.m. EDT on Flight Day 2.
Crew members Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen are reported to be in excellent health.
A minor issue with the spacecraft's waste management system was identified and resolved by mission control and the crew.
The spacecraft has surpassed 100,000 miles from Earth and is maintaining a velocity of approximately 39,428 km/h.
sources: Artemis II Flight Day 3 Mission Update
history.crewStatus: The crew is in excellent health and high spirits; they are currently capturing high-resolution imagery of Earth.
history.lastEvent: Completion of the Translunar Injection (TLI) burn to depart Earth's orbit.
history.nextEvent: Lunar flyby and lunar far-side communication loss scheduled for April 6.

history.headline: Artemis II crew completes Translunar Injection burn, leaving Earth's orbit for the first time in over 50 years.
details: Completed a nearly six-minute TLI burn on April 2 at 7:49 p.m. EDT, accelerating Orion toward the Moon.
The four-person crew successfully resolved a minor blinking fault light on the spacecraft’s toilet system.
Life-support systems were verified as fully functional during a 24-hour High Earth Orbit phase.
Commander Reid Wiseman has begun transmitting high-resolution photography of Earth from the spacecraft.
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Completion of the Translunar Injection (TLI) burn to transition to a lunar trajectory.
history.nextEvent: Lunar flyby at a distance of 4,600 miles beyond the lunar far side on April 6.

history.currentPhase: En route to the Moon on Flight Day 3
history.headline: Artemis II crew and Orion spacecraft, named 'Integrity,' are speeding toward the Moon after a successful translunar injection burn.
details: Mission is proceeding according to plan on Flight Day 3 of its approximately 10-day test flight.
Crew members are in good spirits, actively monitored for health, and using exercise devices.
Orion has deployed solar arrays and completed in-orbit checkouts and maneuvers.
The NASA ARCHeR study is collecting data on crew adaptation to deep space.
history.crewStatus: The four-person crew is in good spirits, actively monitoring their health, and using a flywheel exercise device to maintain physical well-being. NASA's ARCHeR study is assessing their adaptation to deep space.
history.lastEvent: A pivotal five-minute, 50-second Translunar Injection (TLI) burn was executed on April 2, propelling Orion and its crew out of Earth's orbit and onto a trajectory toward the Moon.
history.nextEvent: On Monday, April 6, the crew is expected to surpass the record for the farthest distance humans have ever traveled from Earth.
